prevalence.trend: stable; absolute numbers rising with aging

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute focal neurologic deficit plus CT/MRI confirming ischemia and excluding hemorrhage

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: risk factor screening in primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; rehab access varies

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CT available in referral hospitals; MRI limited
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Thrombolysis within window; thrombectomy in select centers; secondary prevention with antiplatelets/statins
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delayed presentation, limited stroke units, and rehab gaps
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SUS coverage for acute care and secondary prevention
